original_query: select c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice, sum(l_quantity) from customer, orders, lineitem where o_orderkey in ( select l_orderkey from lineitem group by l_orderkey having sum(l_quantity) > 313 ) and c_custkey = o_custkey and o_orderkey = l_orderkey group by c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice order by o_totalprice desc, o_orderdate limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH order_sums AS (
    SELECT l_orderkey, SUM(l_quantity) AS sum_quantity
    FROM lineitem
    GROUP BY l_orderkey
    HAVING SUM(l_quantity) > 313
)
SELECT c.c_name, c.c_custkey, o.o_orderkey, o.o_orderdate, o.o_totalprice, os.sum_quantity
FROM customer c
JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey
JOIN order_sums os ON o.o_orderkey = os.l_orderkey
ORDER BY o.o_totalprice DESC, o.o_orderdate
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replace the IN subquery with a JOIN to a derived table that precomputes the sum, allowing the optimizer to choose a more efficient join method
Eliminate redundant GROUP BY in the main query by reusing the precomputed sum from the derived table
Avoid scanning the lineitem table twice by using the derived table's result in the main query
